steps.title: Map the passage’s structure
steps.content: Sentence 1 states an established hypothesis: Clovis-point spears drove woolly mammoths to extinction. Sentences 2–3 describe an experiment that tests it — a mechanical spear-thrower launched into clay mounds. Sentence 4 gives the result (minimal penetration) and the conclusion: Clovis spears likely weren’t the main cause of extinction. The structure is: established hypothesis $\Rightarrow$ experiment $\Rightarrow$ result that undermines the hypothesis.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice against that structure
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To argue for the significance of new findings amid an ongoing debate among researchers’: The text doesn’t frame this as an ongoing debate or argue for significance; it describes a single experiment and its result. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the method used in an experiment’: The text never evaluates the clay-mound method’s pros and cons; the method is simply described. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To summarize two competing hypotheses and a major finding associated with each one’: There is only one hypothesis in the text; no competing hypothesis is introduced. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To describe an experiment whose results cast doubt on an established hypothesis’: Captures all three elements — the established Clovis-point hypothesis, the mechanical experiment, and the result that undermines it. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
